ghats.name: Pushkar Ghat
ghats.description: Main Pushkaralu bathing ghat — most sacred spot during the festival

ghats.name: Kotilingala Revu Pushkar Ghat
ghats.description: Sacred ghat with Kotilingala temple, important bathing spot

ghats.name: Saraswati Ghat
ghats.description: Named after Goddess Saraswati, peaceful bathing ghat

ghats.name: Gowthami Ghat
ghats.description: Located on the Gowthami branch of Godavari, serene bathing ghat

ghats.name: Tummalapalli Vari Revu
ghats.description: Traditional bathing ghat managed by Tummalapalli family, historic significance

ghats.name: Sri Narasimha Nagar Ghat
ghats.description: Residential colony ghat with good amenities for pilgrims

ghats.name: Dowleswaram Barrage Ghat
ghats.description: Ghat near the famous Dowleswaram Barrage — iconic Godavari landmark

ghats.name: Venkateswara Ghat
ghats.description: Ghat dedicated to Lord Venkateswara, popular among Tirumala devotees

ghats.name: Goshpada Kshetram (Main Kovvur Ghat)
ghats.description: Primary ghat at Kovvur — Goshpada Kshetram, one of the holiest spots across the river

ghats.name: Subrahmanyeswara Ghat
ghats.description: Ghat near Subrahmanyeswara temple at Kovvur

ghats.name: Nagareswaram Ghat
ghats.description: Ghat near the Nagareswara Swamy temple at Kovvur

ghats.name: Purushottapatnam Ghat
ghats.description: Traditional ghat at Purushottapatnam village, near the main festival area

ghats.name: Seethanagaram Ghat
ghats.description: Peaceful ghat at Seethanagaram, good option during peak festival days

ghats.name: Pattiseema Ghat
ghats.description: Ghat at Pattiseema, famous for the Pattiseema Lift Irrigation Project

ghats.name: Vangalapudi Ghat
ghats.description: Serene ghat at Vangalapudi village, less crowded alternative

tourism_spots.name: Papikondalu
tourism_spots.description: Stunning gorge on the Godavari River — often called the 'Grand Canyon of India'. Boat rides through lush hills and tribal villages.

tourism_spots.name: Dindi Resorts
tourism_spots.description: Scenic riverside resort at Dindi, where Godavari meets the sea. Popular for boating, backwaters, and nature walks.

tourism_spots.name: Maredumilli Tourism
tourism_spots.description: Dense forest zone with waterfalls, trekking trails, eco-resorts, and tribal culture — a gateway to the Eastern Ghats.

tourism_spots.name: Polavaram Project
tourism_spots.description: One of India's largest multi-purpose irrigation projects on the Godavari — an engineering marvel and emerging tourist spot.

tourism_spots.name: Kadiyam Nurseries
tourism_spots.description: Asia's largest plant nursery belt, with thousands of varieties of ornamental plants, trees, and flowers along the Godavari banks.

tourism_spots.name: Dowlaiswaram Barrage
tourism_spots.description: Historic barrage built by Sir Arthur Cotton across the Godavari. Iconic landmark, especially beautiful during Pushkaralu.

tourism_spots.name: Godavari Bridge, Rajahmundry
tourism_spots.description: The famous road-rail bridge — one of the longest in Asia — spanning the Godavari, offering panoramic river views.

tourism_spots.name: Sir Arthur Cotton Museum
tourism_spots.description: Museum dedicated to Sir Arthur Cotton at Dowleswaram, showcasing the history of irrigation and the famous barrage.

tourism_spots.name: Damerla Rama Rao Art Gallery
tourism_spots.description: Renowned art gallery in Rajahmundry showcasing Telugu paintings, folk art, and cultural heritage of the Godavari region.

tourism_spots.name: Devi Chowk, Rajahmundry
tourism_spots.description: Vibrant central square of Rajahmundry — bustling with street food, local markets, and cultural energy during the festival.

poojas.name: Pindapradanam
poojas.description: Ritual offering of rice balls (pindas) to departed ancestors — an important ancestral rite performed on the Godavari banks during Pushkaralu.
poojas.significance: Liberates the souls of ancestors; considered highly meritorious during the 12 sacred days.

poojas.name: Mahasankalpam
poojas.description: The grand declaration/vow taken before every major ritual — the pilgrim states their lineage, location, and purpose before God.
poojas.significance: Validates all subsequent rituals; ensures blessings are directed correctly to the devotee's family lineage.

poojas.name: Musivayanam
poojas.description: A special dana (charitable offering) ritual performed by elderly women, gifting household items to priests as an act of devotion.
poojas.significance: Particularly auspicious for women; believed to ensure wellbeing of the family and long life for the spouse.

poojas.name: Ganga Pooja
poojas.description: Ritual worship of the sacred Godavari River, personified as Goddess Ganga — includes lamp offerings, flowers, and Vedic chanting on the river banks.
poojas.significance: Invokes the blessings of the river goddess; the holy bath following this pooja is considered especially sacred.

poojas.name: Gowri Pooja
poojas.description: Worship of Goddess Gowri (Parvati) — performed by married women for the welfare of their husbands and families.
poojas.significance: Bestows marital bliss, prosperity, and fulfillment of family wishes; one of the most popular Pushkaralu rituals for women.
